text:
Claude can change how you work overnight.
Here’s how to master it in one week:

- Download the desktop app and use Cowork mode (not the website) 
- Build one clean context folder with your “About Me” file and anti-AI-style rules 
- Stop writing long prompts, let Claude ask you quick questions and approve the plan 
- Add plugins for marketing, data, or legal tasks 
- Connect your tools (Slack, Notion, Docs) for live context 
- Schedule recurring tasks so Claude keeps delivering even when you’re offline

Result? 80% less prompting time and an AI that finally works.
One focused week of setup and your workflow completely changes.

Who else is using Claude Code in 2026? What’s your biggest productivity win so far? Comment down below.

CHATGPT vs GROK vs GEMINI vs CLAUDE vs PERPLEXITY
Here's which AI tool to use and when:

▪️ChatGPT: Best for creative content, coding, and everyday productivity workflows. Automate your workflow and create custom GPTs for writing, planning, and analysis.

🔺Pro tip: Use ChatGPT to automate your workflow, create custom GPTs for writing, planning, and analysis.

▪️Grok: Best for real-time trends, pop culture, and candid insights. Create viral posts or social commentary with personality and wit.

🔺Pro tip: Grok to create viral posts or social commentary with personality and wit.

▪️Gemini: Best for Google Workspace integration and real-time data access. Use it for organization and collaboration inside Google Workspace to keep everything connected.

🔺Pro tip: Use Gemini for organization and collaboration inside Google Workspace to keep everything connected.

▪️Claude: Best for deep reading, comprehension, and ethical reasoning. Summarize, fact-check, or rewrite large complex documents into simple insights.

🔺Pro tip: Use Claude to summarize, fact-check, or rewrite large, complex documents into simple insights.

▪️Perplexity: Best for verified research, fact-checking, and summarizing sources. Your go-to when accuracy and verification matter.

🔺Pro tip: Use Perplexity when accuracy and verification matter, great for research, reports, and sourcing.

Which one is your go-to? Drop it below.

No guessing. Fewer retakes. Just better shots from the start.

If Claude keeps giving you inconsistent results, your setup is the problem, not Claude.

Here's what separates a messy Claude Code setup from a high-performing one:

▪️ Don't leave CLAUDE.md empty or vague 
Do define your tech stack, architecture, and conventions clearly, it's the first thing Claude reads every session

▪️ Don't mix personal preferences into shared project files 
Do use CLAUDE.local.md for individual overrides so your workflow doesn't break your teammates'

▪️ Don't re-explain your tooling every session 
Do configure mcp.json once, GitHub, JIRA, Slack, databases all connected and version-controlled

▪️ Don't write one giant instruction file for everything 
Do use .claude/rules/ to separate concerns, code style, testing standards, API conventions load contextually

▪️ Don't repeat the same prompts for recurring workflows 
Do create .claude/commands/ slash commands, one keystroke runs your entire review or deploy process

▪️ Don't let Claude operate without guardrails 
Do set up .claude/hooks/ to validate, lint, and block unsafe operations automatically

▪️ Don't overload a single conversation with every task 
Do delegate to .claude/agents/ specialized sub-agents with isolated context for code review, security, and more

Your Claude Code setup is either working for your team or against it. Structure it once. Benefit from it forever.

What's one thing you'd add to this list?

Seedance 2.0 isn’t your average text-to-video tool anymore.
Seedance uses a unified audio-video generation architecture that accepts text, images, audio, and video as inputs all at once. It gives creators director-level control over camera moves, lighting, character consistency, motion physics, and even native synchronized sound, all in one seamless generation.

Have you ever tried using Seedance 2.0?

What does it actually look like to teach a robot how to work?
At this factory in India, workers wear head cameras as they stitch and assemble, recording every motion from their own perspective. 

Many of them are wearing head cameras that capture tasks from their point of view, creating real footage of how hands move, adjust, and assemble in a live production environment.

That footage becomes the raw material for AI training, helping robots understand the nuances of real human movement, not just rehearsed routines.

The future of automation is being built on the factory floor.

Follow @genai.works for more AI breakdowns.

Mark Zuckerberg just dropped a clear vision of the AI future: Most companies won’t train their own giant AI models from scratch.
Instead, they’ll build a custom AI layer on top of shared frontier models (like Meta’s or OpenAI’s).

This layer gets shaped by the company’s products, policies, customer history, and unique way of working,so support, sales, and operations feel truly personal and intelligent, never generic.The real power moves from owning the base model… to owning your own customized intelligence layered on top.

What do you think, will every business soon have its own AI?
